built: Unified POST /api/palace/visualize endpoint connected to Gemini 3.1 Flash
UI buttons for 'Deep AI Cover' and 'Visualize Domain' in the Next.js Dashboard
Native 'mempalace blog list' and 'mempalace blog read latest' commands
Enhanced 'mempalace room show <slug>' to deeply fetch and display recent decisions
decisions: Discarded static keyword prompting for visualizations; required prompt engine to fetch actual historical 'decisions' and 'built' artifacts from related source_memories.
Integrated visualization requests into a single unified endpoint rather than fragmenting by domain.
Bypassed simple markdown rendering in CLI to specifically highlight 'Next Steps' directives in bright cyan/yellow to draw agent attention without breaking them out of terminal context.
next_steps: Review generated Deep AI Cover for the newly drafted blog post.
Test Palace visualization functionality on live production data to gauge accuracy of physical metaphors.
conversation_context: The user noted that early attempts to build cover generation were too simple, relying merely on titles. To meet the standard that 'the image is the project', I implemented a deep context aggregation system that crawls the relational graph (Posts -> source_memories -> actual architectural decisions) and compiles dense, structured visual metaphors before ever calling the image model.
